content:
Kill 8080 port with cmd If you are using Windows, it’s can be useful to kill certain port. I will show you, how to kill the process :
1 2 3 netstat -ano | findstr :8080 taskkill /PID typeyourPIDhere
summary:
Kill 8080 port with cmd

If you are using Windows, it’s can be useful to kill certain port. I will show you, how to kill the process :

1
2
3

netstat -ano | findstr :8080

taskkill /PID typeyourPIDhere
title: Kill 8080

content:
Good Bye PHP5 [14.04.2004-31.12.2018] 14 years of love story with PHP5, now finish, since the new year 2019 ! I started, learned, praticed with this version of PHP. Now PHP5, since December 31, 2018, security support for PHP 5.6.x is officially cease ; marking the end of all support for any version of the ancient PHP 5.x branch 😥
What does it mean ? It exposes hundreds of millions of websites (who still running on PHP 5.x version), to serious security risks ☠️.
what I supposed to do if I still running my website on php 5 ? Read the official documentation of Migrating from PHP 5.6.x to PHP 7.0.x : http://php.net/manual/en/migration70.php
summary:
Good Bye PHP5 [14.04.2004-31.12.2018]

14 years of love story with PHP5, now finish, since the new year 2019 !

I started, learned, praticed with this version of PHP. Now PHP5, since December 31, 2018, security support for PHP 5.6.x is officially cease ; marking the end of all support for any version of the ancient PHP 5.x branch 😥

What does it mean ?

It exposes hundreds of millions of websites (who still running on PHP 5.x version), to serious security risks ☠️.
title: Good Bye PHP5 😥

content:
Render HTML Tags When you store HTML tags in your database, you would like to retrieve these datas as HTML Content, not a string right ?
1 <?php echo $row['description']; ?>` Maybe when you want to display this data, it show as a string character : 1 <ul><li>Hello, I am PHP</li></ul> Don’t worry, call your best elePHPhant 🐘 and use htmlspecialchars_decode()
The htmlspecialchars() function converts some predefined characters to HTML entities
1 <?php echo htmlspecialchars($row['description']); ?> Hope, you enjoy this post 😉
summary:
Render HTML Tags

When you store HTML tags in your database, you would like to retrieve these datas as HTML Content, not a string right ?

1

<?php echo $row['description']; ?>`

Maybe when you want to display this data, it show as a string character : 

1

<ul><li>Hello, I am PHP</li></ul>

Don’t worry, call your best elePHPhant 🐘 and use htmlspecialchars_decode()

The htmlspecialchars() function converts some predefined characters to HTML entities
title: Render Html Tags

summary:
Happy New Year 2019 🎆

Yeah first post of the year. Let me wish you a Happy New Year.
Big project, big work, big ambition, this year will be exciting!

What super fun thing am I planning to do in 2019 ?

  • Read more books 📚
  • Train more ElePHPhant in PHP 🐘
  • Starting to interest in Django Framework 🦎
  • Maintain XPlaneLover✈️ & MyBearIsYellow 🐻 Blog
  • Find a project to contribute in Open Source regularly

That’s the kinda thing, I’m gonna to do…
title: Let's 2019 Begin
